title: Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, Braves’ Mike Yastrzemski, walks off Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Mike Yastrzemski hit a walk-off single for the Braves, leading to a 3-2 win over the Red Sox.
Red Sox pitcher Connelly Early allowed two solo home runs in five innings without any walks.
The Red Sox are struggling, with a record of 18-26 after leaving seven runners on base and batting 1-for-3 with runners in scoring position against the Braves.

title: Report: Reunion between Patriots, Stefon Diggs viewed as unlikely despite mutual interest
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Patriots' reunion with Stefon Diggs is unlikely due to an impending trade for A.J. Brown.
Diggs expressed interest in returning to the Patriots after a 1,000-yard season with them.
Patriots' salary commitments to A.J. Brown and Romeo Doubs complicate adding Diggs to the roster.

title: Boston police seek missing boy, 15
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston police seek help finding Lafayette LaGrant-Gomes, last seen Tuesday near Blue Hill Avenue in Dorchester.
Lafayette LaGrant-Gomes is described as 5'8", with brown eyes and black hair, known to visit local hotspots.
Anyone with information should call 911 or contact District B-3 detectives at 617-343-4712; anonymous tips are accepted.

title: Police: Teen allegedly shot girlfriend’s mother in the head on Mother’s Day
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Tyler Broomes, 18, is accused of shooting his girlfriend's mother and another man on May 10 in Dorchester.
The shooting resulted in the mother's hospitalization with life-threatening injuries; the other victim had non-life-threatening injuries.
Broomes faces multiple charges, including assault with intent to murder, and is held without bail pending a hearing.

title: How would a team made up entirely of former Red Sox look in 2026?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: A hypothetical 2026 roster of former Red Sox includes Mookie Betts, Xander Bogaerts, and Kyle Schwarber, among others.
Former Red Sox pitchers Sale, Eovaldi, and Rodriguez have a combined 2.74 ERA, outperforming current Red Sox starters.
Schwarber leads MLB in home runs, highlighting missed opportunities in free agency for the 2026 Red Sox.

title: Red Sox’ Roman Anthony no longer wearing wrist brace, played catch Friday
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Roman Anthony was placed on the 10-day injured list with a right wrist sprain on May 7.
Anthony has removed his wrist brace and played catch before Friday's game against the Atlanta Braves.
The Red Sox outfielder hopes to resume swinging a bat soon, pending his recovery progress.

title: Jeep drives into Norwood day care
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A Jeep crashed into BrightPath daycare in Norwood, Massachusetts, around 9 a.m. Friday; no injuries reported.
The driver mistakenly hit the accelerator while attempting to stop the vehicle, causing it to hit the daycare.
Daycare temporarily closed for structural inspections; Norwood firefighters reported moderate damage to the building.

title: Here’s what experts are predicting the Patriots’ 2026 record will be
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Experts predict Patriots will end 2026 season with a 10-7 record, lower than last year's 14-3.
Patriots' 2026 schedule includes challenging matches against the Seattle Seahawks, Denver Broncos, and Chicago Bears.
Despite a stronger team, tougher schedule and key away games may hinder Patriots' win total and playoff prospects.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Martin Short documentary 'Marty, Life is Short' is now streaming on Netflix.
'Mars Attacks!' by Tim Burton is available to stream for free on YouTube and Plex.
Season 2 of 'Tucci in Italy' is streaming on Disney+ and Hulu.

title: Mayor Wu remains firm as councilors consider rejecting her budget
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston Mayor Michelle Wu's $4.9 billion budget proposal faces potential rejection from City Council members over spending cuts.
Councilors have until June 10 to accept, reject, or amend Wu's budget, with a potential veto requiring a two-thirds override.
Boston's budget deficit is nearly $50 million, linked to snow removal, police overtime, and health insurance costs.
